headline: LGBTQ Malaysians face 42 state-level criminal provisions
context: A 2025 legal mapping by human rights groups identified 42 provisions across state syariah enactments and federal law criminalising LGBTQ identities and conduct.

cards.big: 42 legal provisions across 14 states criminalise LGBTQ Malaysians
cards.sub: Both Penal Code Section 377 and syariah laws impose criminal penalties

cards.big: Penal Code Section 377A carries up to 20 years imprisonment
cards.sub: Syariah offences add fines up to RM5,000 and caning for Muslims

cards.big: UN Human Rights Council reviewed Malaysia's LGBTQ record in UPR 2024
cards.sub: Malaysia rejected 18 of 20 recommendations on sexual orientation rights

cards.big: Survey of 1,200 LGBTQ Malaysians found 73% experienced workplace discrimination
cards.sub: Mental health crisis documented with 45% reporting suicidal ideation

cards.big: 42 laws target identity, not conduct that harms others
Siti Kasim and 5 lawyers challenged Selangor syariah provisions in 2024
